_meta.title: Refuge — Global Country Reference Data
_meta.description: Complete reference for all 149 states parties to the 1951 Refugee Convention and/or its 1967 Protocol, plus key non-signatory countries with alternative pathways. Global scope — serves displaced people from any origin, going to any destination. This is the authoritative data layer for Refuge's country recommendation system.
sources: DWRAP — World Bank/UNHCR Joint Data Center

tier_system.1: Major active asylum system — high volume, full procedures and contacts (step-by-step registration, required docs, legal aid orgs)
tier_system.2: Active asylum system — moderate volume or significant for specific origin groups, detailed entry
tier_system.3: Functioning system but low volume, primarily transit or origin country — summary entry with strategic note
tier_system.4: Signatory in name only — micro-state, collapsed state, or system non-functional — minimal entry, warn if dangerous

non_signatory_tiers.non_signatory_viable_temporary: Not a signatory but has real alternative pathways (UAE: Humanitarian Permit, Green Residency). Viable waiting country while pursuing formal asylum elsewhere.
non_signatory_tiers.non_signatory_skilled_only: Not a signatory. Viable only for highly skilled with formal employment. No protection framework.
non_signatory_tiers.non_signatory_resettlement_pipeline: Not a signatory. UNHCR present and active. Primary value is third-country resettlement, not local integration.
non_signatory_tiers.non_signatory_not_recommended: Not a signatory and actively hostile, dangerous, or collapsed. Do not recommend as destination.

_meta.convention_intro: The 1951 Refugee Convention and its 1967 Protocol define who is a refugee and outline the legal protection, rights and assistance they are entitled to receive. The cornerstone principle is non-refoulement: a refugee must not be returned to a country where they face serious threats to their life or freedom. To date, 149 States are parties to the Convention and/or its Protocol. Even states that have not acceded are bound by non-refoulement as a norm of customary international law.
_meta.stats_enrichment: All fields marked PENDING are enriched from the UNHCR Refugee Statistics API via data/scripts/enrich_countries.py after running unhcr_downloader.py --all

enrichment_mapping.acceptance_rate_recent: asylum-decisions.json → dec_recognized / dec_total for most recent year
enrichment_mapping.applications_last_year: asylum-applications.json → applied field for most recent year
enrichment_mapping.refugee_population_total: population.json → refugees field, most recent year
enrichment_mapping.data_year: most recent year available in asylum-decisions.json for this country

convention.notes: Encampment policy (Dadaab, Kakuma, Kalobeyei). 2021 Refugee Act improved rights on paper.
Africa's most progressive framework. Self-reliance model. No mandatory encampment.

profile.integration: good — world's most generous refugee policy
profile.economic_access: strong — work and land from arrival

convention.notes: 2019 Refugee Proclamation expanded rights. Internal displacement (Tigray, Oromia).
Increasingly restrictive since 2017. Strict encampment enforced.

profile.integration: poor — strict encampment limits all rights

steps: Apply at DGIE or UNHCR Kigali

convention.notes: UNHCR conducts RSD (delegated). MOU basis. No formal work right. Severe post-2023 backlog.

convention.notes: Protocol only. Implements via Refugee Act 1980. Two tracks: Resettlement and Asylum. 2M+ case backlog. Policies shift with administrations.

AFG.name: Afghanistan

convention.notes: Acceded 2005. World's largest origin country. Taliban takeover 2021 — system non-functional.

AFG.warning: ACTIVE CRISIS. Taliban control since August 2021. 6M+ displaced abroad. World's largest origin country. Do not recommend as destination.

profile.strategic_note: Major origin country. Taliban takeover 2021 — no asylum system. UNHCR Kabul present but severely restricted. Not a destination.

UAE.name: United Arab Emirates

humanitarian_permit.name: Humanitarian Resident Permit
humanitarian_permit.description: Temporary permit for conflict-affected. Requires financial self-sufficiency or sponsor. Healthcare, education, integration support.

green_residency.requirement: skilled worker or freelancer — no employer sponsor needed

UAE.strategic_guidance: Viable temporary country for skilled/educated displaced persons while pursuing formal asylum in a 1951 signatory country. Green Residency removes employer-tie. Humanitarian Permit for conflict-affected. NOT a durable solution — plan onward move.

SAU.name: Saudi Arabia

wusool.description: Talent attraction for skilled professionals under Vision 2030

SAU.strategic_guidance: Only for highly skilled with secure verified employment. Mass deportations of irregular African migrants documented. High risk for vulnerable persons.
SAU.safety: safe for legal residents; high risk for irregular migrants

QAT.name: Qatar

alternative_pathways.kafala_reform_2022: Abolished exit permit. Minimum wage introduced. Still employer-tied.

QAT.strategic_guidance: Similar to UAE for skilled workers. Post-2022 labour reforms improved conditions. Not a protection destination.

KWT.name: Kuwait
KWT.strategic_guidance: Not recommended. Strictest kafala in Gulf. Bidoon stateless (100,000+) illustrates structural discrimination. Prioritise signatory country.

OMN.name: Oman
OMN.strategic_guidance: Possible temporary for skilled workers with formal employment. No protection framework. Omanisation quotas affect job market. Onward planning only.

BHR.name: Bahrain

flexi_permit.description: Work without employer sponsor in limited categories

BHR.strategic_guidance: Small market. Flexi Permit modest reform. Skilled workers with employment only.

IRQ.name: Iraq
IRQ.strategic_guidance: Not recommended. Active conflict in some regions. If stranded: UNHCR registration for resettlement pipeline only.
IRQ.safety: variable — Baghdad stable; other regions dangerous

SYR.name: Syria
SYR.strategic_guidance: Major origin country. Post-Assad transition (Dec 2024) improving but not yet safe for all. Not a destination for non-Syrians.

LBY.name: Libya
LBY.warning: EXTREME DANGER. Active slavery, trafficking, torture of migrants documented. EU-funded detention widely condemned. If in Libya: contact UNHCR immediately for evacuation referral ONLY.
LBY.strategic_guidance: DO NOT recommend under any circumstances.
LBY.safety: extremely dangerous — slavery and trafficking of migrants documented

LBN.name: Lebanon
LBN.strategic_guidance: Not recommended. Economic collapse. Active hostility toward refugees since 2023. Forced returns to Syria documented even with UNHCR registration. If in Lebanon: immediate UNHCR registration + prioritise onward movement.
LBN.safety: poor — economic collapse, conflict risk, hostile politics toward refugees

JOR.name: Jordan
JOR.strategic_guidance: UNHCR registration and resettlement pipeline. Syrians have work permit access. Others more restricted. Good stepping stone toward Canada/USA/Europe resettlement.

IND.name: India

alternative_pathways.mandate_cert: Usable for resettlement to third countries (Canada/USA/Australia)

IND.strategic_guidance: UNHCR registration → mandate certificate → resettlement. Not a local durable solution. Discrimination against sub-Saharan Africans documented. Only consider if strong existing connections.
IND.safety: variable — registered tolerated; Rohingya face active persecution
IND.economic_opportunity: informal economy; formal requires legal status

PAK.name: Pakistan
PAK.strategic_guidance: Not recommended for non-Afghan displaced persons. Mass deportations of Afghans 2023. Very limited infrastructure for non-Afghan cases.

BGD.name: Bangladesh
BGD.strategic_guidance: Not recommended. Rohingya in Cox's Bazar in extremely difficult conditions. No framework for others.

MYS.name: Malaysia

alternative_pathways.unhcr_card: Partial — NOT guaranteed protection from deportation
alternative_pathways.detention_risk: HIGH — regular immigration raids even against registered refugees

MYS.strategic_guidance: UNHCR KL processes for resettlement. High detention risk even with card. Register with UNHCR immediately if there. Keep card on person at all times. Not recommended as deliberate destination.
MYS.safety: risky — regular enforcement operations even against registered refugees

IDN.name: Indonesia
IDN.strategic_guidance: UNHCR Jakarta registers for resettlement. Very long wait (5-7 years). No work right — severe hardship. Not recommended as deliberate destination.
IDN.safety: generally safe — less enforcement than Malaysia

THA.name: Thailand
THA.strategic_guidance: UNHCR resettlement pipeline only. High detention risk. Do not recommend as deliberate destination. If there: urgent UNHCR registration. Keep UNHCR documentation on person at all times.

CHN.name: China

convention.note: SIGNATORY but near-0% recognition rate. Forcibly returns North Korean defectors. Effectively non-implementing.

CHN.strategic_guidance: Not recommended despite formal signatory status. Near-0% recognition. North Korean defectors actively returned. Skilled employment visa possible if highly educated — not protection.
CHN.note_on_classification: Formally a signatory (1982) but listed here due to effective non-implementation
CHN.safety: safe for legal residents — surveillance state
CHN.economic_opportunity: high for skilled professionals with legal status

RUS.name: Russia

convention.note: SIGNATORY but active war in Ukraine since 2022, very low recognition, hostile environment.

RUS.strategic_guidance: Not recommended. Active war in Ukraine. Hostile political environment. Low recognition. Not a practical destination.
RUS.note_on_classification: Formally a signatory but listed here due to current war and impracticability
RUS.safety: not recommended — active war zone risk, hostile politics toward foreigners

SGP.name: Singapore
SGP.strategic_guidance: Only for highly educated with verified employment at professional salary. Immediate detention for undocumented. Viable temporary stay while pursuing formal asylum in a signatory country.
SGP.safety: very safe for legal residents — strict enforcement for undocumented

CUB.name: Cuba

convention.notes: Convention only. Non-implementing. Major origin country.

CUB.strategic_guidance: Not recommended. No asylum framework. Cuba is itself a major origin country. Authoritarian state.
CUB.note_on_classification: Cuba acceded to the 1951 Convention (1954) but has no functional asylum system and is itself a major origin country. Listed here accordingly.

ERI.name: Eritrea
ERI.strategic_guidance: Not recommended. Major origin country. Forced conscription and political repression. Closed authoritarian state. No asylum framework.

MNG.name: Mongolia
MNG.strategic_guidance: Not recommended for most displaced persons. Remote, extreme climate, very limited infrastructure.
